'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Nuribe-jinja}} [[JA:漆部神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11565409}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=August 2020}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Nuribe Shrine | Image = [[File:Nuribe-jinja (Ama, Aichi) VLux5 hdsr 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ama City|Q451661|WD=Q451661|1=Ama, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Jimokuji (place name in Ama City)|Q28154635|WD=Q28154635|lt=Jimokuji|1=Jimokuji, Ama}} Higashimonzen 11 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Mimishukune{{Cite Kotobank|word=Nuribe Shrine|encyclopedia=|accessdate=2024-06-23}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}
{{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Unknown (before the {{ill|lt=15th century|Q7018|WD=Q7018|1=15th century}}) | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = October 18 | Rituals = | Alternative names = Hachidai Myōjin{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}
Hachidai Shrine
Hachidai Sha | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nuribe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Nuribejinja1.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Approach to the shrine]] '''Nuribe Shrine''' (ぬりべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Ama City|Q451661|WD=Q451661|1=Ama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Jimokuji (place name in Ama City)|Q28154635|WD=Q28154635|lt=Jimokuji|1=Jimokuji, Ama}} Higashimonzen. It is adjacent to {{ill|lt=Jimokuji Temple|Q4160865|WD=Q4160865|1=Jimoku-ji Temple}}. There is a theory that identifies it with the “Nuribe Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Ama District (Aichi Prefecture)|Q685886|WD=Q685886|lt=Ama District|1=Ama District}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}. === History === Until the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, it was called '''Hachidai Myōjin''' and served as the tutelary shrine of Jimokuji Temple. Today, due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the precincts are divided. The date of foundation is unknown. The oldest record is a {{ill|lt=historical document|Q89298567|WD=Q89298567|1=komonjo}} from {{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 7 (1498), in which eight divine names—“Kamo, Sumiyoshi, Kasuga, Hirano, Gion, Matsuo, Kibune, Inari”—and their {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji|1=UNKNOWN}} are written in ink{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}. Records from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} state that 20 koku from the Jimokuji estate were allocated as ritual funds for this shrine. Several {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} (ridge tags) from the Edo to Shōwa periods survive, suggesting that the shrine buildings were repaired multiple times. In the mid-Edo period, a theory was proposed identifying this shrine with the “Nuribe Shrine” of Ama District listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. The basis for this identification was the presence nearby of a mound called Urushi-zuka (lacquer mound){{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}. However, there is no conclusive evidence for this identification, and {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}}, who once identified this shrine as Nuribe Shrine, later refuted his own theory{{Sfn|Yahagi Village History Compilation Committee|2000|pp=750–755}}. Other candidate shrines for Nuribe Shrine include Ichigami Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsushima City|Q816736|WD=Q816736|1=Tsushima, Aichi}} and Asama Shrine in {{ill|lt=Nakagawa Ward|Q1155226|WD=Q1155226|1=Nakagawa-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Shimoisshiki-chō (Nagoya)|Q62017174|WD=Q62017174|lt=Shimoisshiki-chō|1=Shimonoisshiki-chō}}, among others. Scholarly opinion had not settled on an identification, as works such as the “Owari Province Shikisha Zadokumoku” state “Nuribe Shrine: location unknown”{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}. The {{ill|lt=Tokusen Shinmeichō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}} considers the Engishiki shrine “Nuribe Shrine” of Ama District as follows: {{Quotation|At present, considering Shiokiri, one might suspect this shrine to be Tsushima Shrine; another theory says it is Tsushima Ichigami Shrine, but there is no further proof. There is also a theory that it is Hachiman Shrine of Ninoe Village in the same district, suggesting that “Ninoe” might be read as “Nuribe,” but this is doubtful. Another claim says that the priests who served the Mitarai River of Asama Shrine in Shimoisshiki Village of Aichi District were connected to the Nuribe River, but investigation shows that this has been separated since ancient times and is not persuasive. There is also a theory that Jimokuji Kannon is a transformed form of this shrine, but this too lacks evidence and cannot be accepted. At present, it should be considered an abolished shrine.|“Tokusen Shinmeichō,” Owari Province, Ama District}} Thus, there was also a theory that the Engishiki Nuribe Shrine had become an abolished shrine{{Sfn|Shikinaisha Commendation Society|1987|pp=93–105}}. However, because the theory that this shrine attached to Jimokuji was the Engishiki Nuribe Shrine spread from the Edo period onward, it was officially renamed Nuribe Shrine in {{ill|lt=1957|Q5311|WD=Q5311|1=1957}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 32). In recent years, another theory has been proposed that Nuribe Myōjin was enshrined at an Ebisu Shrine that once existed in {{ill|lt=Aisai City|Q269644|WD=Q269644|1=Aisai}}, {{ill|lt=Kawakita-chō (Aisai City)|Q131900621|WD=Q131900621|lt=Kawakita-chō|1=Kawakita-chō}}{{Sfn|Yahagi Village History Compilation Committee|2000|pp=750–755}}. === Enshrined deities === * Mimishukune ** Said to be the fifth generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Amateru no Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Honoakari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Nuribe (people engaged in the production of lacquerware). * {{ill|lt=Konohana-sakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohana-sakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * Hachidai Myōjin (a collective term for the deities of {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine|Q1306606|WD=Q1306606|1=Kamo shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Gion Shrine|Q11588442|WD=Q11588442|1=Gion shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Kibune Shrine|Q276779|WD=Q276779|1=Kifune Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Hirano Shrine|Q738971|WD=Q738971|1=Hirano Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}}) === Location === * 11 Higashimonzen, Jimokuji, Ama City, Aichi Prefecture === Transportation === {{Commonscat|Nuribe-jinja}} * About a 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Jimokuji Station|Q6201690|WD=Q6201690|1=Jimokuji Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tsushima Line|Q6810431|WD=Q6810431|lt=Tsushima Line|1=Meitetsu Tsushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://ama-kankou.jp/page_culture/nuribejinjya/ Nuribe Shrine] – Ama City Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nuribe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nuribe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nuribe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ama]] [[Category:History of Ama]]
